mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.5 percent signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer purchasing power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and business demand, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-expansion periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and slowing manufacturing output, triggering short-term risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of declining INDPRO observations, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4 percent raises expectations of central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and generates negative forward market returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses statistical significance after adjusting for concurrent changes in the federal funds rate.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls supports aggregate demand and corporate hiring capacity, which translates into higher earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of positive payroll growth months, or the Rank IC drops below 0.02 across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant quarterly GDP growth combined with rising unemployment confirms a broad-based demand contraction, which depresses corporate profit margins and leads to negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over individual series, or 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of contractionary periods during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above the 95.0 threshold signals manufacturing sector recovery and improved capacity utilization, generating short-term positive equity momentum as investors price in higher output.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 65% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 50% after controlling for concurrent monetary policy announcements.
